titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the ABCFM Daguerreotypes Collection
titlestmt.author: Yale Divinity Library staff

controlnote.p: This collection contains 82 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 82 digital files.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 5, 2025 at 18:02

corpname.part: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Collection transferred from Andover Newton Theological School in 2017

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Information on complete names, life dates, dates of service, and geographic areas of mission stations of the missionaries is from the published guide to the microfilm collection, Papers of The American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions, and from biographical sketches compiled by John Adams Vinton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains daguerreotype images of missionaries who had been appointed for service by The American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions. The daguerreotype process for creating photographs was used during the 1840s and 1850s. Series III contains two color miniature portraits that date from 1839. This collection was originally held at the Franklin Trask Library of Andover Newton Theological School and was transferred to the Yale Divinity Library in 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I: Photographs of Missionaries appointed by the American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
Series II: Photographs of Other Individuals and Scenes

corpname.part: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions

did.unittitle: Photographs of Missionaries Appointed by American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
